cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the information from the passage. The article mentions that even bits of space junk as small as dots of paint pose a threat. Then it explains that this is because the debris orbits Earth at speeds up to 28,200 km per hour. The high speed is why tiny paint dots have damaged space shuttle windshields so badly that they needed replacement. So the key point here is the high speed of the debris. The answer should relate to their speed. Let's check the options again. Option A says they travel at a very high speed. That matches exactly with what the passage states. Option B is about being difficult to track. The passage doesn't mention tracking difficulty as the reason; it focuses on the speed causing damage.
